layers.name: Delta (CRDT)
layers.description: Operational delta and CRDT primitives (consensus clocks, core deltas, graph/index deltas, WASM bridge) used to encode incremental state changes across the cluster.

layers.name: Replication & Raft
layers.description: Raft consensus, replication transport, snapshot management, and cross-replica coherence keeping cluster state durable and consistent.

layers.name: Lake & RAIRS
layers.description: Long-term storage substrate: RuLake data lake and RAIRS retrieval/indexing service backing analytical and archival reads.

layers.name: Tiny Dancer Routing
layers.description: Tiny Dancer routing fabric (core algorithms, node runtime, WASM client) directing requests and shard traffic across the cluster.

layers.name: Verified & Acorn
layers.description: Verified-compute layer combining Verified attestation primitives and Acorn proof/execution crates (native and WASM) for trustworthy computation.

layers.name: Kalshi Integration
layers.description: Kalshi prediction-market integration crate exposing market data and event signals to the cluster.

tour.title: Cluster Overview — the 12 crates
tour.description: Slice 10e is the distributed storage cluster of RuVector. It groups twelve Rust crates into six layers: CRDT deltas, Raft replication, the RuLake/RAIRS storage tier, Tiny Dancer routing, the Verified+Acorn trust layer, and the Kalshi prediction-market integration. This first stop names every top-level crate so later steps slot into a known map.

tour.title: Delta family — CRDT primitives
tour.description: Every state change in the cluster is encoded as a delta. ruvector-delta-core defines the algebraic delta (compose, inverse, identity), while delta-consensus carries vector/Lamport clocks, delta-graph and delta-index apply deltas to graph and ANN structures, and delta-wasm exposes the same primitives to browser clients. Understanding deltas first is essential — replication, snapshot, and coherence all transport them.

tour.title: Replication and Raft consensus
tour.description: Deltas only become durable cluster state once Raft agrees on them. ruvector-raft implements leader election and log replication; ruvector-replication is the transport that ships committed deltas to followers; ruvector-snapshot compacts the log into restorable snapshots; ruvector-coherence enforces cross-replica invariants. Together they keep the cluster consistent under churn.

tour.title: Lake & RAIRS — long-term storage substrate
tour.description: Below the hot Raft path sits the analytical/archival tier. ruvector-rulake is the object-store-backed data lake (with a filesystem backend for tests and federation smoke-tests). ruvector-rairs layers retrieval and indexing on top of the lake — IVF clustering, k-means training, and the SEIL/RAIRS train pipelines convert raw lake contents into searchable vector indexes.

tour.title: Tiny Dancer — FastGRNN routing fabric
tour.description: Tiny Dancer decides where each request goes. The core crate trains a FastGRNN model (model.rs, training.rs) which the Router consults at runtime; admin-server.rs exposes the routing decisions and health/readiness over HTTP; optimization.rs quantizes weights to int8 for fast inference; circuit_breaker.rs trips the router away from unhealthy shards. This is the data-plane brain of the cluster.

tour.title: Verified & Acorn — gated proofs over fast indexes
tour.description: ruvector-verified turns each search/insert into an attested computation. FastTermArena is a pooled term store, the gated tier router (route_proof / verify_tiered) chooses cheap vs. strong proofs per request, and pipeline.rs composes verification stages. ruvector-acorn provides the filtered ANN engine that Verified gates — AcornIndex and FilteredIndex implement the actual k-NN search the cluster serves.

tour.title: Kalshi — prediction-market signal source
tour.description: ruvector-kalshi is the cluster's outbound integration to the Kalshi prediction market. auth.rs (Signer/SignedHeaders) does RSA-PSS request signing, rest.rs and ws.rs/ws_client.rs handle REST and websocket streams, rate_limit.rs enforces per-route caps, normalize.rs reshapes Kalshi messages into internal events, and strategy_adapter.rs plus brain.rs (BrainClient/SharedMemory) bridge those events back into the cluster's decision logic. This is the final step because it sits on top of every layer below it.
